quoteFirst: We were gradually ascending, and reached at about
quoteLast: o’clock the summit of a rising ground the soil of which consisted of red clay. Altogether it was an undulating country, appearing rather monotonous, from its almost total want of trees, but nevertheless of the highest interest to one just arrived from the arid regions of the north.
title: Travels and discoveries in North and Central Africa

quoteFirst: Did you see your little son to-day emulating the antics of Nature’s aerial ornithopters? I left Aerodrome “B” about
quoteLast: and went over to S., then I branched off at right angles for W., but as I was about 4,000 feet up I could not pick it out from the other parks and commons, and so, finding myself running into a formidable set of clouds, I “about turned,” and after taking my map from my pocket and studying it on my knee for a few minutes, I found out where I was and set out for Aerodrome “A.

quoteFirst: At length the stranger returned, and the first sound that greeted his ears as he let himself in and stood within the dimly lighted passage was a prolonged and unmistakable snore.  Now in the said passage there stood a clock--a large timepiece of venerable aspect--and the hands of this clock were close upon a
quoteLast: .  Whether it was a kind of instinct that prompted him to do so, or that he noticed that the glass was closed, but not shut--or both--cannot be here recorded, but in a moment he had opened the clock face, put the hands back forty minutes, and, having closed the glass again, proceeded carelessly in the direction of the snore.

quoteFirst: At last I was sure that it must be almost nine, so I asked Mr. Avery if he'd please take me home; and don't you believe, when we got there, it was
quoteLast: , and papa was coming for me!  Dorry said he guessed I must be enjoying myself to stay so late.  I didn't tell anybody about it for three days, because I knew they'd laugh at me, and they did.
title: What Katy Did at School

quoteFirst: Hanssen, who had driven first the whole way, without a forerunner, and for the most part without a compass, had no need to be ashamed of his performance. We agreed that it was well done, and that, no doubt, was all the thanks he got. We reached it at
quoteLast: , and unloaded our sledges at once. Wisting undertook the far from pleasant task of getting us a cup of warm milk at -68.8° F.
